statusLine: EU fuel security is under significant strain with 13 of 15 countries reporting critical stock levels in at least one fuel type, while oil prices surge to $109/barrel amid Middle East supply disruptions.
fullAnalysis:
The European Union faces a deteriorating fuel security situation with widespread critical stock shortages across member states. While EU averages remain near the 90-day mandatory minimum (petrol 94.5 days, diesel 95.8 days, jet fuel 89.9 days), this masks severe disparities at the national level. Thirteen of the fifteen reporting countries show critical stock levels in at least one fuel category, with Romania, Poland, and Spain experiencing critical shortages across all three fuel types.

The situation is particularly acute for diesel and jet fuel. Diesel stocks are critical in 10 countries, with Romania holding just 20.2 days of supply—less than a quarter of the required minimum. Jet fuel presents the most widespread concern, with 12 countries below critical thresholds and the EU average at 89.9 days, dangerously close to the mandatory floor. Only Germany maintains safe petrol stocks at 107 days, while Latvia's diesel reserves of 113.9 days represent the sole bright spot in diesel coverage.

The fuel crisis is compounded by soaring oil prices, with Brent crude reaching $109.03/barrel—a 7.78% weekly increase that reflects broader Middle East supply disruptions. This 27% price surge since the conflict began is driving consumer fuel costs higher, with petrol averaging €1.789/L and diesel at €2/L across the EU. The combination of depleted strategic reserves and elevated prices suggests European fuel security remains highly vulnerable to further supply shocks.
keyPoints: 13 of 15 EU countries report critical fuel stock levels in at least one category, with Romania, Poland, and Spain critical across all fuel types
Jet fuel situation is most severe with 12 countries below critical thresholds and EU average at just 89.9 days
Diesel stocks critical in 10 countries, including Romania at catastrophically low 20.2 days of supply
Brent crude oil surged 7.78% this week to $109.03/barrel, up 27% since Middle East conflict began
Only 2 countries maintain safe stock levels in any fuel category: Germany (petrol) and Latvia (diesel)
